servinfo.title: Engine Oil and Filter Change

item.ptxt: New and used engine oil can be hazardous.
Be sure to read “WARNING” in General Precautions and observe what is written there.
Step 1) – 8) must be performed with ENGINE NOT RUNNING.
For Step 9), be sure to have adequate ventilation while engine is running.

servinfo.ptxt: Before draining engine oil, check engine for oil leakage.
If any evidence of leakage is found, make sure to correct defective part before proceeding to the following procedure.

s1.ptxt: Drain engine oil by removing drain plug (1).
After draining oil, wipe drain plug clean. Reinstall drain plug with new gasket and tighten drain plug to specified torque.
Loosen oil filter using special tool and remove oil filter.
Wipe clean oil filter mounting surface on oil pan.
Apply engine oil to new oil filter gasket.
Install new filter on oil filter stand by hand until filter O-ring contacts mounting surface.

attention2.ptxt: To tighten oil filter properly, it is important to accurately identify the position at which filter O-ring first contacts mounting surface.

s1.ptxt: Tighten oil filter (1) 3/4 of a turn (2) from point of contact with mounting surface using special tool.

Replenish oil until oil level is brought to FULL level mark (hole) on oil level gauge (oil pan and oil filter capacity). Oil filler port is at top of cylinder head cover. It is recommended to use engine oil of SG, SH, SJ, SL or SM grade. Select appropriate oil viscosity according to engine oil viscosity chart [A] below.
SAE 0W-20 oil is the best choice for good fuel economy and good starting in cold weather.

item.ptxt: Engine oil capacity is as specified below.
However, note that actual amount of oil required during oil change may somewhat differ from that indicated below depending on such factors as temperature, viscosity, etc.
Engine oil specification and viscosity chart [A] indicated in this manual are typical ones.
For the specific information on the engine oil and proper engine oil viscosity, refer to “Engine Oil and Filter” section of the Owner's Manual.

s1.ptxt: Start engine and run it for 5 minutes. Stop it and wait another 5 minutes before checking oil level. Add oil, if necessary, to bring oil level to FULL level mark (hole) (1) on oil level gauge.
Check oil filter and drain plug for oil leakage.
